2604.14886.title: Cooperate to Compete: Strategic Data Generation and Incentivization Framework for Coopetitive Cross-Silo Federated Learning
2604.14886.paper_summary: In data-sensitive domains such as healthcare, cross-silo federated learning (CFL) allows organizations to collaboratively train AI models without sharing raw data. However, practical CFL deployments are inherently coopetitive, in which organizations cooperate during model training while competing in downstream markets. In such settings, training contributions, including data volume, quality, and diversity, can improve the global model yet inadvertently strengthen rivals. This dilemma is amplified by non-IID data, which leads to asymmetric learning gains and undermines sustained participation. While existing competition-aware CFL and incentive-design approaches reward organizations based on marginal training contributions, they fail to account for the costs of strengthening competitors. In this paper, we introduce CoCoGen+, a coopetition-compatible data generation and incentivization framework that jointly models non-IID data and inter-organizational competition while endogenizing GenAI-based synthetic data generation as a strategic decision. Specifically, CoCoGen+ formulates each training round as a weighted potential game, where organizations strategically decide how much synthetic data to generate by balancing learning performance gains against computational costs and competition-caused utility losses. We then provide a tractable equilibrium characterization and derive implementable generation strategies to maximize social welfare. To promote long-term collaboration, we integrate a payoff redistribution-based incentive mechanism to compensate organizations for their contributions and competition-caused utility degradation. Experiments on varying learning tasks validate the feasibility of CoCoGen+. The results show how non-IID data, competition intensity, and incentives shape organizational strategies and social welfare, while CoCoGen+ outperforms baselines in efficiency.
2604.14886.paper_summary_zh: 在資料敏感的領域，如醫療保健，跨孤島聯邦學習（CFL）允許組織在不共享原始數據的情況下協作訓練 AI 模型。<lb/>然而，實際的 CFL 部署本質上是合作競爭的，組織在模型訓練過程中合作，同時在下游市場中競爭。<lb/>在這種情況下，訓練貢獻，包括數據量、質量和多樣性，可以改善全球模型，但卻不經意地加強了競爭對手。<lb/>這一困境因非獨立同分佈（non-IID）數據而加劇，導致不對稱的學習收益，並削弱持續參與的動力。<lb/>雖然現有的競爭意識 CFL 和激勵設計方法根據邊際訓練貢獻來獎勵組織，但它們未能考慮加強競爭對手的成本。<lb/>在本文中，我們介紹了 CoCoGen+，這是一個兼容合作競爭的數據生成和激勵框架，該框架共同建模非 IID 數據和組織間競爭，同時將基於 GenAI 的合成數據生成內生化為一個戰略決策。<lb/>具體而言，CoCoGen+ 將每個訓練回合公式化為一個加權潛力遊戲，組織根據學習性能收益與計算成本及競爭引起的效用損失之間的平衡，戰略性地決定生成多少合成數據。<lb/>然後，我們提供了一個可處理的均衡特徵，並推導出可實施的生成策略以最大化社會福利。<lb/>為了促進長期合作，我們整合了一種基於收益再分配的激勵機制，以補償組織的貢獻和競爭引起的效用下降。<lb/>在不同學習任務上的實驗驗證了 CoCoGen+ 的可行性。<lb/>結果顯示，非 IID 數據、競爭強度和激勵如何塑造組織策略和社會福利，而 CoCoGen+ 在效率上優於基準。
2604.14886.author: Thanh Linh Nguyen et.al.
2604.14886.authors: Thanh Linh Nguyen, Nguyen Van Huynh, Quoc-Viet Pham
